text:
As the days are ticked off in the run-up to what will be my finalOlympicsinParis, I’ve taken the opportunity  to pause for thought and look at what’s going on around me in the British team.

In less than two months, I’ll be competing on that great stage for the last time before contemplating a retirement that will hopefully allow me to stay connected and do good work withingymnasticsbut also give me time to explore new avenues and spend more time with my family.

So it was with this in mind that I agreed to take part in a content series for Team GB ahead of the Olympics, interviewing and speaking to some of my fellowBritish athletesas host.

I have to admit, while there was so much to look forward to and while I was keen to hear from these great competitors, I’m not a huge watcher of sport in general away from gymnastics so I needed to throw myself into my research before speaking toswimmerTom Dean, 400metre runner Laviai Nielsen and BMX freestyler Kieran Reilly.

I’d also never hosted anything like this, as it’s a first for Team GB with this series of athlete-on-athlete interviews, so it was a real departure for me and one which was both daunting as well as exciting.

There’s a broader theme here too, which was important for me, of wanting to not only shine a light on these athletes, but to highlight the positive spin on how they overcome their everyday challenges.

We intended the series to be a deep dive into the lives of athletes, charting their careers but also the challenges that come with competing for your country at the very top of your game and the pinnacle of your chosen sport.

There were things in their lives that these guys had never opened up about before and it was intriguing for me to be on the other side of the fence, the interviewer instead of the interviewee for once, host instead of guest.

I was able to find a bit more information about Tom and Laviai but it was tougher with Kieran, who there is less about online.

However, me and him hit it off and we were able to capture some really cool content of things that haven’t been done before: me performing somersaults while he pulled off some of his flips, moves and tricks on camera. I was truly out of my comfort zone.

I’m fascinated by the different approaches people take to their careers – it was of great interest to hear how Tom, Laviai and Kieran’s processes related to mine and hopefully they still have more Olympics ahead of them once I have called it a day.

I hadn’t crossed paths with Laviai before but there was almost an instant click there with each of them. There was a rapport, fortunately, a sense that we have all been on a very, very similar journey.

See Max Whitlock OBE host this newYouTubeseries presented by Team GB and Young Minds, on Team GB’s YouTube channel, in the lead-up toParis2024

It was also great to learn more about how different sports work and I have to thank all three of them for being so open and honest in our chats.

Tom, especially, was very frank about the criticism and comments he had aimed at him after stating – quite openly – that he wants to bring back multiple medals from Paris.

Tom, of course, was so good in the pool in Tokyo where he won two golds and goes to France looking for another big performance but with different expectations now, particularly from the outside.

There were lots of differences but also similarities in our experiences. I’m excited for it all to come together and to see the series come out.

It was so good to hear from athletes who are masters of their craft. The series has really given me a nice sideline to focus on ahead of what is sure to be an emotional summer for me. I hope you enjoy the finished product.

Each of the trio have massive targets for the Games and I feel like I’m on the journey with them to Paris.

title: I'm stepping out of my comfort zone before Paris 2024 - my final Olympics | Metro News
description: Max Whitlock talks about interviewing fellow Olympians ahead of the Paris Olympics, which will be his last as a competing gymnast.
keywords: Sport,Columnists,First Person,Max Whitlock,Metro newspaper,Olympics,Real Life,Team GB
